title: tapisciaceae
intro: Tapisciaceae is a family of flowering plants that are primarily found in East Asia, characterized by their unique adaptations and characteristics. This family encompasses a range of species, each with distinct features such as leaf shape and flower arrangement. The tapisciaceae family is notable for its relatively small number of species, with around 100 to 150 known species classified under this rank. Members of this family are often shrubs or small trees with a unique growth habit, and are of interest to botanists and horticulturists for their ornamental and medicinal properties.
